name: English Muffins 3
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Unsalted Butter, cut into small pieces and softened
recipeInstructions:
Sponge:
1. Butter a 4-quart mixing bowl.
2. In a small bowl, combine warm water and sugar. Sprinkle yeast over water mixture. Let stand until yeast dissolves and bubbles.
3. While yeast is dissolving, in the buttered bowl combine warm milk, butter, and honey. Stir until butter is almost melted.
4. Stir in 2 1/2 cups flour and dissolved yeast. Beat on high speed for 3 minutes.
5. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled, about 30 minutes.
Dough:
1. Butter a 4-quart bowl.
2. Stir salt into bread mixture. Stir in enough of the remaining flour with a wooden spoon until dough forms a ball.
3. Turn dough out onto a well-floured surface. Knead until smooth and elastic, about 8-10 minutes, adding enough of the remaining flour to keep dough from sticking.
4. Place dough in buttered bowl, turning once to butter side up. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled, about 45 minutes.
5. Punch down dough. Divide dough into half.
6. Sprinkle work surface generously with cornmeal. Turn dough out on board. Sprinkle cornmeal over top of dough.
7. Gently roll each half of dough to 1/2-inch thickness. Cut with a floured biscuit cutter into 3-inch rounds. Repeat with remaining half of dough.
8. Knead, reroll, and cut dough scraps once, if desired. Arrange dough rounds on ungreased baking sheets 2 inches apart.
9. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled, about 30 minutes.
10. Heat a lightly oiled, nonstick griddle or skillet over medium-high heat.
11. Lift some of the dough rounds with a spatula onto griddle. Cook for 2 minutes on each side.
12. Reduce heat to medium. Cook, turning every few minutes, until done in center, about 13-18 minutes.
13. Cool on racks. Repeat cooking remaining muffins.
14. Split muffins crosswise with the tines of a fork.
